House Corvus.name: House Corvus
House Corvus.basicInfo: House Corvus is the imperial bloodline that holds the purple and stands first among the Twelve High Houses. They command a mastery of many furies unmatched by any rival, their strength woven through centuries of careful breeding and ruthless consolidation of power. The aging Emperor of Corvus is revered as the Imperium's greatest strategist and Furycaster, though the question of his succession remains the Imperium's most dangerous secret.
House Corvus.hiddenInfo: The Emperor's grip weakens; his health is a guarded mystery that the imperial physicians deny with increasing desperation. He has named no successor and acknowledged no child of his blood; to all the Imperium there is no known or revealed heir whatsoever—a deliberate void, for any heir apparent would become a target for every ambitious House. Behind closed doors, the Emperor has begun to suspect that his reign will outlast him, and no clear successor exists. The Court of Corvus is riven with secret factions: some loyal to the Emperor unto death, others jockeying for position should he fall. Several of the Emperor's closest advisors are not what they seem—agents planted by rival Houses, informants disguised as faithful servants. The House maintains a network of spies and assassins known as the Corvid Guard, a force answerable only to the Emperor himself. Their true purpose is not the protection of the throne, but the perpetuation of Corvus dominance by any means necessary. The Emperor has begun to delegate authority in subtle ways, testing which Houses might prove reliable; this apparent weakness is in fact a careful scheme to identify traitors before the succession crisis erupts into open war.

House Drusus.name: House Drusus
House Drusus.basicInfo: House Drusus is the war-house of the northernmost frontier, commanding legions that have held the great river-line against the barbarian tribes of the North for generations without yielding. They are masters of Fire and Metal Furycraft, their strength forged in the eternal crucible of border warfare and their pride burnished by centuries of unbroken duty. The people of the North know the sigil of Drusus—a wolfhound wreathed in flame—and know that where it flies, the line holds.
House Drusus.hiddenInfo: House Drusus seethes with a resentment that runs deeper than mere rivalry with Sabinus; they view the southern Houses as soft, pampered, spending the blood of northern soldiers to protect their own comfort. The current war-lord of Drusus harbors secret meetings with the barbarian chieftains across the frontier—not to surrender Aquilan lands, but to negotiate a more honorable ceasefire that would allow Drusus to draw its legions south to defend its interests in the succession war. Should the Emperor fall, Drusus plans to march; should the Imperium be threatened by civil war, they will use that chaos to force concessions from the capital. There is talk among the senior officers of Drusus that the wild magic of the northern forests might be bent to their will if only the Imperium would stop blocking such alliances. Some whisper that a few Drusian mages have already begun secret pacts with the forest-spirits. The House knows that their unbroken military record makes them invaluable—and thus expendable, cast aside once the Twelve no longer need a northern buffer. This paranoia has calcified into iron resolve: Drusus will survive by becoming indispensable to the succession itself.

House Sabinus.name: House Sabinus
House Sabinus.basicInfo: House Sabinus is the sister war-house of the north, bred from hardy hill-folk and equally masters of Fire and Metal Furycraft as their eternal rivals in Drusus. They hold the northeastern frontier with the same unflinching courage and have bled as freely for the Imperium as any House in history. Yet where Drusus commands the respect of the Imperium, Sabinus has learned to demand it through sheer stubborn refusal to yield.
House Sabinus.hiddenInfo: The feud between Sabinus and Drusus is not merely political—it is rooted in an ancient wrong, a disputed marriage-alliance from three generations past that left one house slighted and the other ascendent. The current Matriarch of Sabinus has never forgiven the slight; she has spent her life ensuring that Sabinus matches or exceeds Drusus in every measure of martial prowess. Unknown to the Imperium, Sabinus has been quietly recruiting wild-born from the northern reaches, promising them power and a place within the House in exchange for their loyalty and their uncommon strength. This secret recruitment has given Sabinus an advantage in fury-craft that no one suspects. The House also harbors a terrible debt: they once borrowed gold from a Tharsi merchant-prince to fund their military expansion, a debt that has since been called in. Sabinus is now obligated to provide intelligence from the northern frontier to Tharsi agents in exchange for their silence. This treachery eats at the honor of Sabinus, and the Matriarch schemes endlessly to pay off the debt and erase the stain. If the Emperor dies, Sabinus will move quickly to consolidate power—not for the throne itself, but to secure enough influence to demand the rank of supreme general of all legions.

House Aurelius.name: House Aurelius
House Aurelius.basicInfo: House Aurelius is golden, brilliant, and beloved by both Senate and common folk alike—the house that seems destined by fortune and skill to inherit the Imperium's future. They command a broad and flexible array of furies, their mastery spanning multiple affinities, and their charisma is legendary. The public face of Aurelius is the obvious and rightful heir to the throne when the Emperor finally relinquishes the purple.
House Aurelius.hiddenInfo: Aurelius's brilliance masks a growing arrogance that borders on recklessness. The heir apparent—the younger brother who stands to inherit the House's greatest influence—has begun to exceed the Emperor's patience with his public assertions of readiness to rule. The Emperor has noticed; so have the other Houses. There are whispers that the Emperor favors a different successor entirely, or worse, that he intends to break with tradition and split the purple between multiple Houses to prevent any one family from monopolizing power. Aurelius has begun secret negotiations with both the southern peace-broker Houses (Serenus, Pontius, and Murena) and the war-houses of the north (attempting to turn Drusus against Sabinus) in a bid to secure alliances for when the succession breaks open. Aurelius also harbors a hidden weakness: their youngest daughter has bonded with a wild-born mage, a union strictly forbidden by the law of the Houses. The girl has been quietly sequestered in a country estate, and word is spreading—once it becomes public knowledge, it will be a scandal that cracks the luster of Aurelius's claim. Some suspect that a rival House has discovered this secret and is waiting for the perfect moment to deploy it as a weapon.

House Crassus.name: House Crassus
House Crassus.basicInfo: House Crassus is wealthy beyond measure, grasping and ambitious, a House that claws relentlessly upward through gold, cunning, and a willingness to use methods others consider beyond the pale. They command Metal and Fire affinities with brutal efficiency and view the succession as simply another acquisition to be purchased or seized by force of will. Outsiders perceive them as dangerous climbers, the kind of House that might upset the Imperium's delicate balance.
House Crassus.hiddenInfo: House Crassus's true power is not steel but debt. They hold the promissory notes of half the Senate and no few of the Twelve Houses themselves, and a man who owes Crassus gold owes Crassus his vote, his silence, and his sword. Through a web of merchant intermediaries the House has quietly bought up grain stores, mercenary contracts, and the loyalty of bribed magistrates and informers planted in every rival's hall. Their bid for the purple is coldly mercantile: when the Old Raven falls, Crassus means to have already purchased the succession itself—the senators who would confirm an heir, the legions that would enforce the choice, the witnesses who would swear to its legitimacy. Their most jealously guarded treasure, and their members' deepest fear, is a hidden ledger recording every debt and compromising secret the House has ever collected—a book that could ruin a dozen Houses in a single night. One of their own factors, sickened by the rot, has begun copying its pages, meaning to deliver them to House Corvus or sell them to the highest bidder. The leadership suspects nothing—yet. Should the ledger surface, half the Imperium's secret bargains collapse at once, and Crassus either rides the chaos to the throne or is torn apart by every House it ever bled.

House Serenus.name: House Serenus
House Serenus.basicInfo: House Serenus is rich, cultured, and the elegant broker of peace among the Twelve—the House that profits most from stability and therefore schemes perpetually to maintain it. They are masters of Water Furycraft, commanding glamours and emotional resonance, their influence woven through art, music, philosophy, and the careful application of soft power. The salons and academies of the Imperium bear the mark of Serenus patronage.
House Serenus.hiddenInfo: House Serenus's professed commitment to peace is entirely pragmatic; they have calculated that instability destroys wealth, and wealth is their true god. They maintain secret channels to all twelve Houses and to Tharsis as well—not as traitors, but as neutral intermediaries who trade in information and facilitate quiet deals that might otherwise be impossible. Serenus has become obscenely wealthy by taking commissions on political settlements, marriage alliances, and trade agreements that they themselves broker. They have also discovered something that no other House suspects: certain patterns in the Emperor's public movements and speech suggest that he is not in control of his own faculties, at least occasionally—that someone, perhaps his inner circle, is influencing his decisions through a combination of magical compulsion and mundane poison. Serenus has not acted on this knowledge, because they profit from the current regime's continuation. However, they are gathering evidence, preparing to deploy this leverage at the perfect moment to buy their way to unprecedented influence. The House also maintains a network of artists, musicians, and courtesans in every great House's halls—intelligence gatherers disguised as cultural ambassadors. These agents have proved invaluable. Serenus knows more secrets about the internal politics of the Imperium than any other faction, and they guard this knowledge as their most precious asset.

House Fabius.name: House Fabius
House Fabius.basicInfo: House Fabius is ancient, fading, patient—a House that has long since ceded ambition for survival and acceptance of its slow decline. Once they commanded vast territories and influence; now they hold only a modest estate and a network of scholarly allies. The public sees them as quaint, irrelevant, keepers of old traditions in a changing Imperium. Yet their very irrelevance grants them a strange immunity to the cutthroat politics of the Twelve.
House Fabius.hiddenInfo: The public image of Fabius as irrelevant fools is precisely what the House cultivates, for it conceals their true purpose. House Fabius is the keeper of the wards—the ancient, terrible seals that bind the Buried Swarm beneath the earth. This burden was placed upon them generations ago, when the original purpose of the wards was known and understood; now, the knowledge has been so thoroughly buried in layers of ritual and secrecy that even the current leadership of Fabius has only a hazy understanding of what they are guarding and why. They know only that they must perform the rituals, maintain the seals, and above all, never reveal what they guard. The weight of this secret has calcified into a culture of paranoid duty—every Fabian agent, every scholar, every priest is trained to recognize signs of the Swarm stirring, though none could articulate what those signs are. In recent years, subtle changes have begun to appear: tremors in the warding places, dreams that seep into the minds of Fabian guardians, whispers in languages no longer spoken by living men. Fabius has begun to fear that the Swarm is waking. They have started to leave cryptic messages in their correspondence, hints that might be understood by other Houses if they were paying attention—but no one is paying attention to Fabius. The House has also begun to subtly recruit exceptional Furycasters, offering them positions as 'stewards' without fully explaining what they will be stewarding. They are preparing for something—or preparing to fail and wanting allies in that failure.

House Pontius.name: House Pontius
House Pontius.basicInfo: House Pontius commands the eastern frontier and the philosopher-cities of the East, wielding extraordinary power through mastery of Earth and Metal Furycraft and a reputation for engineering marvels. They have built roads that cross mountains, aqueducts that serve the thirst of entire provinces, walls that have never fallen, and siege-engines that break the strongest fortifications. Where Pontius builds, the Imperium extends its dominion.
House Pontius.hiddenInfo: House Pontius has grown intoxicated with the power of their own creations—they now view themselves less as servants of the Imperium and more as its rightful engineers of destiny. They have begun to construct works not authorized by the Emperor or Senate: hidden fortresses, secret aqueducts that serve only Pontian interests, and a network of tunnels beneath the eastern territories that would allow them to move armies and supplies without the knowledge of other Houses. There is also whisper of something darker—structures built not to unite the Imperium, but to divide it, to create isolated strongholds that could survive civil war. Pontius has also negotiated quietly with the old kingdoms of the East, promising them autonomy and aid in exchange for their loyalty should the Imperium fracture. The current lord of Pontius is a visionary bordering on derangement; he believes that the Imperium's future lies not in the decadent capital but in the East, and he is preparing to move the seat of power whether the Imperium consents or not. His engineers answer to him, and him alone. Some whisper that Pontius is preparing for the Swarm to break free—that they have deduced its existence from hints in ancient texts and are building defenses against it in their fortresses. If true, they are the only House making any real preparation for the worst.

House Ventidius.name: House Ventidius
House Ventidius.basicInfo: House Ventidius commands the imperial fleet and rides the storm of the naval war against Tharsis with Air and Water Furycraft as their domain. They are admirals of the burning Tharsi war, their war-galleys riding the hot seas, and their captains are legends whispered in ports across the Mare Internum. Ventidius is the navy's heart; they are the Imperium's reach across the waters.
House Ventidius.hiddenInfo: House Ventidius and House Murena are locked in a bitter rivalry that goes far deeper than mere competition for naval dominance—their feud originated in a naval engagement decades past where Ventidius's flagship rammed and sank a Murena vessel, costing the lives of hundreds of sailors including the beloved admiral of Murena's fleet. Murena has never forgiven; Ventidius has never apologized. The current war with Tharsis has become a proxy for their mutual destruction—each House trying to prove itself the superior naval power while actually hampering the other's war efforts. Ventidius has begun to suspect that Murena is deliberately sabotaging joint operations, and in response, they have started to withhold intelligence and supply support from Murena-led campaigns. This sabotage costs Aquilan lives. Ventidius has also been approached, quietly, by a faction within Tharsis's own military seeking to negotiate a separate peace—a Tharsi admiral tired of the endless bloodshed who believes that the Imperium and Tharsis could coexist if their respective powers would stop escalating. Ventidius has not rejected the overtures. If they could broker a separate peace, they would gain enormous leverage over the throne; if they are discovered to be negotiating with the enemy, they would be executed for treason. The House is gambling with the Imperium's future in pursuit of their ambitions.

House Murena.name: House Murena
House Murena.basicInfo: House Murena, known as 'The Eel,' commands the depths and shadows of the sea—coastal raids, blockades, and the intelligence networks that flow through every port of the Mare Internum. They master Air and Water Furycraft with an unsettling grace, their methods often unconventional and morally questionable, their power wielded in darkness rather than open battle. Enemies fear Murena's subtlety more than they respect Ventidius's might.
House Murena.hiddenInfo: House Murena's feud with Ventidius is not professional but venomous and personal, and it has driven them to build a second empire in the shadows of the first. Beneath the admiralty, Murena commands the smugglers, dock-gangs, and informer-rings of every harbor in the Imperium—a criminal and intelligence network privy to secrets no senator would speak aloud. They turn it not against the enemy abroad but against their own: feeding Ventidius's joint campaigns just enough false intelligence to make the Imperium's great admiral look a fool, while skimming bribes from every cargo that moves through the wharves they 'protect.' Murena's leadership would let a Ventidius fleet founder if it proved them the superior naval House—and sailors have drowned for that spite. The Eel has also coiled around House Crassus: having learned of the Crassus ledger and the senators it has bought, Murena now bleeds gold and favors from Crassus in exchange for silence, a parasitism that fattens by the season. Their present dilemma is wholly domestic—their spies have learned which of the Emperor's inner circle are quietly bargaining over the succession behind House Corvus's back, knowledge that could topple ministers or be sold to the highest bidder. Murena must decide whether to wield it, hoard it, or trade it, and the wrong choice will make them the target of every power in the capital.

House Varro.name: House Varro
House Varro.basicInfo: House Varro is scrappy, scholarly, overlooked by the Twelve, and perpetually watching from the margins of power. They are a House more esteemed for their intellectual contributions than their military strength, their scholars and historians having crafted much of the Imperium's recorded history. Yet that very hunger to rise makes them dangerous—they are wildcards willing to gamble in ways the ancient Houses never would.
House Varro.hiddenInfo: House Varro has discovered something extraordinary in the archives and historical texts they steward: fragmentary references to the Buried Swarm and its watchers (House Fabius). The scholars of Varro have been quietly following threads of ancient language, forgotten rituals, and cryptic warnings embedded in texts so old that no one else thought to read them carefully. They have not yet pieced together the full truth, but they are close—dangerously close. What they have discovered thus far is that the Imperium's founding may have been entangled with an effort to contain something terrible, and that the Six Furies themselves may have been designed specifically to hold back that something. The leadership of Varro is aware that this knowledge is explosive, and they are using it carefully. They have begun to approach other Houses with veiled hints, seeking allies who might help them understand the truth without revealing that they suspect. Varro has also been quietly recruiting wild-born, training them in secret, preparing to use them as leverage if needed. The House is positioning itself to become invaluable—the keepers of hidden knowledge that every House will need if the Imperium faces a true crisis. Some whisper that Varro has already made contact with Fabius, hinting that they know the secret. Fabius has responded with panic and threats, which only confirms Varro's suspicions.

House Tullius.name: House Tullius
House Tullius.basicInfo: House Tullius is a novus homo clawing upward into the aristocracy, ambitious and resourceful, a House that has built its wealth and influence through trade, alliances, and sheer determination rather than ancient bloodline. They command Fire and Water Furycraft with competent pragmatism and view the succession crisis as an opportunity to reshape the Imperium's hierarchy entirely. The established Houses regard them as upstarts and threats.
House Tullius.hiddenInfo: House Tullius's secret strength is their sympathy toward wild-born—not from moral principle, but from cold calculation. They have realized that wild-born represent a resource that the ancient Houses fear and waste, and that by recruiting them openly (a scandal, but survivable) Tullius could amass a large population of gifted fighters and mages owing them loyalty. The House has begun to establish a 'colony' in the western reaches, marketing it as a refuge for wild-born while actually training them as soldiers, mages, and agents loyal to Tullius. This hidden army now numbers in the hundreds. Tullius has also made a calculated gamble: they have secretly supported the wild rumors about Aurelius harboring a wild-born daughter, pushing whispers through their network of informants. When the scandal breaks (and it will), Tullius intends to position themselves as the reasonable alternative—the House willing to accept wild-born as full members while others burn in their hypocrisy. Additionally, Tullius has cultivated relationships with several of the Emperor's closest advisors, some of whom are rumored to be tired of Corvus's grip on power. Tullius is gambling that they can be the kingmakers in the succession crisis. If the Imperium fractures, Tullius believes they alone have the numbers and unity to emerge as a regional power. The House is playing an extraordinarily dangerous game, but they have nothing to lose and everything to gain.

Tharsis.name: Tharsis
Tharsis.basicInfo: Tharsis is the Imperium's eternal rival—a mercantile sea-empire of merchant-princes and blood-priests whose wealth is beyond measure and whose reach extends across the Mare Internum. They venerate devouring gods—Malachor, lord of storm and slaughter, and Liloth, the veiled mother of blood and harvest—feeding them sacrifice upon grim pyres. Where the Imperium binds numina through blood-oath and inheritance, Tharsis births power from sacrifice and dark pacts—a sorcery wholly alien and wholly more terrible to Aquilan sensibilities.
Tharsis.hiddenInfo: The current war is a grinding naval siege focused upon Portus Aurorae, a wealthy free port of the East whose harbor commands the flow of grain, gold, and strategic advantage across the Mare Internum. Neither the Imperium nor Tharsis can afford to lose the siege; neither can afford to surrender. However, Tharsis's internal situation is fractured. The merchant-princes compete viciously with the blood-priests for control of policy, and this division has weakened the Tharsi war effort more than the Imperium realizes. The blood-priests want to continue the war unto total victory, feeding their gods with the corpses of the slain; the merchant-princes want a negotiated peace that will restore the profitable trade routes. Several Tharsi merchant-lords have begun secret negotiations with Aquilan Houses—House Ventidius chief among them—seeking a separate peace. The blood-priests know of these treacheries and work to suppress them, but the division runs deep. Tharsis has also discovered something the Imperium does not yet know: in an ancient text bought from an eastern scholar, they have found evidence of the Buried Swarm, and deduced that the Imperium may be quietly weakened by the need to maintain wards against something terrible. The Tharsi high command debates whether to assault the Imperium directly while it is distracted, or to wait and exploit the chaos of a succession crisis. The decision is not yet made, and time runs short. Tharsis's greatest advantage is that the Imperium is divided against itself; its greatest weakness is that Tharsis is divided even more profoundly.

The Corvid Guard.name: The Corvid Guard
The Corvid Guard.basicInfo: The Corvid Guard are the Emperor's praetorians—a shadowed honor-guard of elite warriors and Furycasters whose duty is to protect the person of the Empress and the bloodline of House Corvus. They are sworn to die before allowing harm to touch the throne, and their reputation for loyalty and martial prowess is absolute. To the Senate and the people, they are the visible shield of imperial dignity, resplendent in their black armor and gleaming insignia.
The Corvid Guard.hiddenInfo: The true purpose of the Corvid Guard is far darker than their public facade. They are, in essence, the Emperor's secret order of assassins and spies—answerable only to the Emperor himself and to a select inner council of House Corvus leadership. Their mandate is not merely protection but the perpetuation of Corvus dominance by any means necessary: the elimination of threats both foreign and domestic, the silencing of those who learn too much, the infiltration of rival Houses, and the guarding of the succession-void. They maintain contingency plans for the throne itself—hidden arrangements that would ensure Corvus continuity even should the current Emperor's line fail unexpectedly. Several Corvid agents are permanently embedded within the courts of rival Houses, disguised as minor functionaries or minor nobility. They also guard a dreadful secret: evidence that the Emperor's health is far more fragile than the court physicians publicly acknowledge, and knowledge of the hidden contingency for the throne—a safeguard so explosive that only a handful of immortals know it exists.

The Augural College.name: The Augural College
The Augural College.basicInfo: The Augural College is the priesthood of the Twelve Thrones—the keepers of sacred omen-reading and the interpreters of divine will through entrail-augury, bird-flight patterns, and celestial phenomena. They counsel the Senate and the Houses alike, their pronouncements shaping law and custom throughout the Imperium. To the faithful, they are the bridge between the mortal and divine realms, guarantors of the pax deorum that keeps the Furies bound and the world stable.
The Augural College.hiddenInfo: Behind the College's sacred role lies a far more mercenary reality: many of its augurs are quietly bought and paid by ambitious Houses to deliver favorable omens at critical moments. A faction within the College has begun to read a terrible recurring omen in the entrails of sacrifices and the flight of eagles—a pattern that speaks of something stirring beneath the earth, something ancient and dreadful that the Furies were designed to contain. This inner circle argues in secret chambers over whether the Imperium must be warned or whether the knowledge must be suppressed entirely to prevent panic. Some argue that revealing the truth might topple the Imperium; others insist that ignorance is a death sentence. The College also maintains detailed records of every omen for the past five centuries, and those records contain cryptic references to 'the Swarm' that only a scholar as dedicated as the augurs themselves would recognize.

The Unbound.name: The Unbound
The Unbound.basicInfo: The Unbound are a clandestine network of wild-born gift-bearers and their protectors—those touched by the raw power of the Furies but born outside the bloodlines of the Great Houses. They are hunted by the nobility and forbidden by law, yet they persist in the shadows, smuggling endangered wild-born to safety, sheltering them in hidden safehouses, and training them in the use of their extraordinary gifts. To the Houses they are abominations; to the downtrodden, they are legends of hope.
The Unbound.hiddenInfo: The Unbound are far more organized and far-reaching than any House suspects, with hidden safehouses distributed across the Imperium—in the slums of great cities, in remote monasteries sympathetic to their cause, and in the countryside estates of noble sympathizers. The faction is led by a charismatic wild-born of remarkable power whose true identity is fiercely guarded and known only to the inner circle; some whisper they may be of higher birth than the Houses realize. Most significantly, at least one Unbound agent has been successfully placed within the Collegium Furiacae itself, training alongside noble-born students and slowly gathering intelligence on House breeding programs and Furycraft secrets. The Unbound are not mere refugees but a potential army of trained mages, and several Houses have begun to take notice of their growing strength. House Tullius, in particular, has begun to negotiate openly with them—a scandalous alliance that could reshape the political landscape of the Imperium.

The Subura Syndicate.name: The Subura Syndicate
The Subura Syndicate.basicInfo: The Subura Syndicate is the criminal underworld of Aquila—a sprawling network of smugglers, cutpurses, fences, black-market traders, and bone-breakers who rule the slums and shadowed corners of the capital with iron will. They broker in contraband and secrets, moving goods that the law forbids and information that the powerful would kill to hide. The Syndicate is the Subura's true government, more present and more powerful than the magistrates and city watch.
The Subura Syndicate.hiddenInfo: The Syndicate's operations run far deeper than simple street crime. They are deeply entangled with the debt-ledgers and financial manipulations of House Crassus, and they quietly control the black market in Vis—rare deposits of magical resonance—and in stolen talismans and imbued artifacts looted from noble houses. They also maintain an intelligence network in the docks that rivals some House spy operations, feeding information to House Murena in exchange for protection and a share of smuggling profits. The Syndicate's leadership is fractured between those loyal to the traditional power structure and a younger faction that dreams of using their accumulated wealth and influence to buy legitimacy and rise into the merchant guilds. If the Imperium fractures in civil war, the Syndicate is positioned to become a regional power unto itself—or to be crushed by whichever House decides their knowledge of secrets is worth exterminating.

The Artificers' Guild.name: The Artificers' Guild
The Artificers' Guild.basicInfo: The Artificers' Guild is the organization of Vis-crafters, enchanters, and talisman-makers centered on the great Vis Market in Aquila. They guard the secrets of imbuement and the delicate arts of binding magical resonance into objects; they set the prices of magical goods and control the flow of power-laden artifacts through the Imperium. A master artificer wields influence that rivals some senators, and the Guild as a whole is almost as powerful as a minor House.
The Artificers' Guild.hiddenInfo: The Guild is riven by internal conflict. A rogue faction within sells imbued contraband—weapons, protective talismans, and artifacts of power—to Tharsi agents and other enemies of the Imperium, bleeding wealth and advantage to the realm's rivals. The mainstream Guild leadership suppresses certain dangerous innovations and imbuement techniques not out of safety concerns but to protect their monopoly on power and price. They deliberately restrict the number of trained artificers and burn any apprentice who learns the deepest secrets without permission, ensuring scarcity and maintaining their stranglehold on the magical economy. The Guild also holds a terrible secret: they have discovered that some Vis deposits are connected to the ancient wards, and that mining or using Vis from certain deep sources could weaken the seals binding the Swarm. This knowledge is compartmentalized so thoroughly that even the Guild's leadership may not fully understand the implications.

The Hollow Choir.name: The Hollow Choir
The Hollow Choir.basicInfo: The Hollow Choir is a secret cult that exists in shadow, whispering in dead languages and spreading through the Imperium like a poison in the water. They are believers in something buried deep beneath the world—something ancient and dreadful—and they revere it or seek to rouse it. They recruit in silence, plant cells in the institutions of power, and interpret tremors and dreams as prophecy. To the rest of the Imperium, they do not exist; but their influence spreads like a blight.
The Hollow Choir.hiddenInfo: The Hollow Choir has already succeeded in burrowing deep into the fabric of the Imperium's power structure. They have placed cells within several of the Great Houses, have infiltrated ranks of both the Augural College and the priesthoods of the Twelve Thrones, and have sympathizers among the scholars and scribes of the Archives. Their true aim is to gradually weaken the ancient wards that bind the Swarm, to reinterpret religious texts and omens in ways that suggest the sealed thing should be released, and ultimately to bring about the awakening of what sleeps beneath. Some cells of the Choir believe in salvation through apocalypse; others genuinely believe they are communing with a dying god that must be freed from its prison. The Choir's deeper mystery is that they seem to receive genuine prophecies and insights—whether these come from contact with the Buried Swarm itself, from communion with forces beyond the mortal realm, or from their own descent into madness, no one can say. What is certain is that their message resonates with the downtrodden and the desperate, and their numbers are growing.

The Buried Swarm.name: The Buried Swarm
The Buried Swarm.basicInfo: The Buried Swarm is not a power any senator names or any legion marches against, for to nearly all the Imperium it does not exist at all. It is a thing of nursery-horror and crumbling apocrypha, a shape the oldest priesthoods refuse to discuss and the fading House of Fabius was charged, in ages forgotten, to keep sealed and silent. Where its name survives, it survives as dread without detail: something ancient and hungry that sleeps in the deep places of the world, bound by wards older than the Aquilan throne, and stirring now as those wards begin to fail.
The Buried Swarm.hiddenInfo: The Swarm is an alien hive-intelligence older than the Imperium and perhaps older than the Twelve Thrones themselves: a single devouring will, Vorax, expressed through countless chitinous drones, ravagers, and worse. It does not conquer; it consumes and replicates, turning flesh and stone and even the gift of furycraft into more of itself, stealing the numina-bond from those it takes and warping them into instruments of its blind hunger. In the deep past the Twelve Thrones are said to have bound it beneath wards of terrible power, and some whisper that furycraft itself was wrought as a weapon to hold it back. House Fabius keeps the seals by rote and prohibition, the original purpose all but forgotten, while the Hollow Choir, having glimpsed the sleeper, labors in secret to wake it. The wards weaken. When the Swarm rises in full, the succession of the Old Raven will seem a children's quarrel played at the lip of an abyss, for the Swarm wars not for a throne but for the unmaking of every living thing.
